meaning: vt.璧疯瘔,鎺у憡,鎸囨帶,鍛婂彂
examples: The ICC must decide whether to indict the suspects.
You can't indict whole people for the crudeness of a few.
Their decisions on whether to indict someone have mostly been unappealable.
All she needs in order to indict is probable cause that a crime has been committed.
To wax eloquent about someone's embossed silver, sumptuous carpets or marble statuary was to indict him.
